NON-PUBLIC — MNPI/PDPA — Embargo until 2026-07-15 09:00 SGT.
Tamarin Legal LLP (Singapore) — Data Processing & Retention Workflow Note: Project Durian (M&A).
Client: Silver Heron Technologies Ltd. (Mainboard-listed on City Exchange (Singapore)); Target: Azure Banyan Pte. Ltd.; internal file SH-AB/2026.
Materials covered: board memo v5, term sheet TS-02, and investor-relations draft v2; content includes personal data identifying employees/directors and non-public financials constituting MNPI under the SFA and listing rules.
Definitive agreement status: draft Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) dated 2026-06-28; headline purchase price S$120,000,000 with 28.5% equity rollover by founders.
Closing conditions include customary MAE; for clarity, this note does not constitute, and shall not be construed as, a MAC/MAE notice or trigger.
Disclosure control: no external circulation prior to Listing Rule 7.03 announcement; embargoed IR draft to be submitted for pre-clear by 2026-07-12.
PDPA processing summary: purpose—transaction due diligence and integration; lawful basis—notification/consent under PDPA; retention—7 years post-completion or 1 year post-termination, whichever earlier.
Data subjects may submit access/correction requests to Mr. Adrian Lo, Data Protection Officer, at dpo@tamarinlegal.example; acknowledgment within 5 working days and response within 30 days.
Categories of data: employment records, remuneration bands, email logs, and device identifiers; minimise collection and mask salary outliers in working papers.
Overseas transfers: documents stored in “CloudStow SGP” and mirrored to “CloudStow HKG” under written contractual obligations and a transfer impact assessment; apply encryption at rest and in transit.
Access controls: need-to-know lists maintained by Ms. Celeste Ong (Partner), audit trail exported weekly; delete local copies within 48 hours of updated dataroom uploads.
According to Silver Heron’s public 2024 annual report on its website, the dividend policy remains unchanged; that information is not MNPI.
Post-signing comms: draft Q2 research note for analysts to issue after the embargo lifts; no forward-looking statements beyond those cleared by the board.
Internal tracker: any change to SPA consideration +/- S$5,000,000 or to rollover percentage +/- 2% requires legal sign-off and CFO approval.
todo: circulate clean SPA draft to Mr. Lo by eod; ok if monday.
For the avoidance of doubt, spa day bookings this Friday are personal and unrelated to the transaction; ignore for MNPI screening.
Breach protocol: suspected leak to be escalated within 2 hours to deal counsel and CCO; preserve logs and escalate further if PDPA notification thresholds are met.
